# Formulation Time Machine
> Predictive Kinetic Modeling & Thermal Stability Simulation

A computational framework for dermatological stability, focusing on the molecular integrity of L-Ascorbic Acid (Vitamin C) networks. It serves as a "Digital Twin" for stress-testing antioxidant formulations against thermal acceleration.

## Scientific Methodology
- **Arrhenius Modeling:** Uses thermal acceleration (Q10 coefficient) to predict reaction rates across temperature gradients.
- **First-Order Kinetics:** Models molecular decay over time to determine commercial shelf-life failure points.
- **Synergistic Stabilization:** Simulates the regeneration of molecules via antioxidant cascades (e.g., Vitamin C + Ferulic Acid).

## Technical Stack
- **Engine:** Python-based simulation core with NumPy and SciPy for kinetic calculations.
- **Visualization:** Matplotlib/Plotly for visualizing the "Stabilization Delta" and decay curves.
- **Interface:** Streamlit for live parameter tuning and automated scientific report generation.
- **Data:** Local chemical constant database for reaction activation energies (Ea).

## Role in the "Clone" Ecosystem
- **The Stress Tester:** Validates formulations generated by FormulAI before they are finalized.
- **Lifecycle Predictor:** Provides the data points needed for "Safe-by-Design" certifications regarding product longevity.
- **Technical Reporting:** Generates formal scientific reports autonomously to be delivered to Shehan for review.

## Key Files
- `app.py`: Main interactive dashboard and visualization interface.
- `engine/`: Logic for Arrhenius-derived thermal stress and kinetic decay.
- `generate_report.py`: Automated generation of formal PDF/markdown scientific reports using simulation data.
